caseStudy.title: Lakehouse Migration
caseStudy.description: Consolidating data lake and warehouse into a unified platform.
caseStudy.overview: A financial services firm operated separate data lake and data warehouse infrastructure that had diverged over five years, resulting in duplicated datasets, inconsistent transformation logic, and a two-tier analyst experience where different teams worked from different versions of the truth.
caseStudy.problem: Data engineers spent significant time keeping the lake and warehouse in sync. Discrepancies between the two systems caused recurring conflicts in board-level reporting. The dual infrastructure had a combined annual cost exceeding $8M.
caseStudy.solution: A lakehouse migration was executed, consolidating all analytical workloads onto a single open-format platform. Existing ETL jobs were migrated and refactored to use declarative transformation frameworks. A medallion architecture was adopted to enforce data quality gates at each processing stage.

results.summary: The consolidated lakehouse eliminated the data consistency issues that had plagued the firm for years. Data engineers now focus on building new capabilities rather than maintaining synchronization between systems.
